text: Which of the following describes the behavior of a 'Standard' multi-cluster warehouse scaling policy?

options.a: Starts additional clusters only when the system estimates enough load to keep them busy for 6 minutes.
options.b: Starts additional clusters immediately to minimize query queuing.
options.c: Resizes the warehouse T-shirt size automatically based on query complexity.
options.d: Shuts down the warehouse if no queries are run for 5 minutes.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows you to share data with a consumer who does not have a Snowflake account by creating a 'Reader Account' for them.
What is the primary purpose of 'Micro-partitions'?

options.a: To allow users to define their own partitioning logic.
options.b: To enable automatic data pruning during query execution.
options.c: To store data in a row-based format.
options.d: To manage the metadata of the Cloud Services layer.

text: Which role is recommended for managing Snowflake users and roles?
What is the maximum Time Travel retention period for a 'Transient' table?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpipe' uses serverless compute resources, meaning you are not billed for a customer-managed warehouse.
Which of the following are valid sources for an 'External Stage'? (Choose three.)
What happens to a running query if the virtual warehouse size is changed (e.g., Small to Medium)?

options.a: The query is aborted and must be restarted.
options.b: The query continues to run on the original resources until completion.
options.c: The query immediately moves to the larger resources.
options.d: The warehouse is suspended and restarted.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Pipes' in a database?
True or False: Data shared via 'Direct Sharing' is only available to accounts in the same cloud region.
What is the primary benefit of 'Time Travel' in Snowflake?

options.a: Faster point-in-time recovery of modified or deleted data.
options.b: Automatic archiving of data to cheaper storage tiers.
options.c: Increasing query performance for analytical workloads.

text: Which Snowflake component handles 'Metadata Management' and 'Query Optimization'?
What is the default character for field delimiting in the Snowflake COPY command?
True or False: All data stored in Snowflake is automatically encrypted at rest.
Which command is used to upload a file from a local filesystem to a Snowflake internal stage?
What is 'Zero-copy cloning'?

options.a: Metadata is duplicated, but actual data micro-partitions are shared until modified.
options.b: The data is physically copied to a new storage bucket immediately.
options.c: It is a feature only available for databases, not tables.
options.d: It requires a 'Large' warehouse to execute.

text: Which view is used to see the credit consumption of a specific virtual warehouse?
True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the individual user level.
What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.b: To restrict row visibility in a table based on user role and data attributes.
options.c: To manage the number of rows per micro-partition.
options.d: To encrypt specific rows of data.

text: Which Snowflake edition is the minimum required for 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'?
True or False: 'Temporary' tables in Snowflake persist across multiple user sessions.
Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization Service'?

options.a: It speeds up point lookup queries on large tables by using a persistent metadata structure.
options.b: It is a free tool in all Snowflake editions.
options.c: It is used for searching files within a Snowflake internal stage.
options.d: It replaces the need for clustering keys.

text: What is the primary benefit of the 'Snowflake Marketplace'?

options.a: Organizations can discover and access third-party data directly without ETL or data movement.
options.b: It provides a place to buy hardware for cloud data centers.
options.c: Users can buy and sell Snowflake credits.
options.d: It is the only way to download Snowflake drivers.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Users' in your Snowflake account?
True or False: You can apply a 'Network Policy' to an individual user to override the account-level setting.
What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.a: Deleting old data to save storage costs.
options.b: The process where Snowflake skips reading micro-partitions that it knows do not contain data relevant to the query.
options.c: Reducing the size of a virtual warehouse.
options.d: Removing metadata from the cloud services layer.

text: Which role is required to manage 'Organizational' tasks like creating multiple Snowflake accounts?
True or False: Snowflake handles the automatic 'Rotation of encryption keys' for its customers.
Which Snowflake component provides the results for a query if it was recently run and the data has not changed?
What happens to credit consumption when a virtual warehouse is in a 'Suspended' state?

options.a: It continues at a lower rate.
options.c: It is charged a flat monthly fee.

text: True or False: A 'Transient' table persists across sessions but has no Fail-safe period.
Which command is used to download data from a Snowflake stage to a local machine?
What is the primary benefit of 'External Tables'?

options.a: They provide faster query performance than standard tables.
options.b: They allow you to query data sitting in cloud storage without loading it into Snowflake.
options.c: They do not require a virtual warehouse to query.
options.d: They have 90 days of Time Travel by default.

text: Which Snowflake edition is the first to include 'Column-level Security' features like Dynamic Data Masking?
True or False: You can apply a 'Masking Policy' to a column in a Snowflake 'View'.
What does the 'ON_ERROR = CONTINUE' parameter do in a COPY INTO command?

options.a: It stops the load at the first error encountered.
options.b: It skips files with errors and continues loading valid data from other files.
options.c: It retries the load automatically until successful.

text: Which role is primarily used for creating 'Warehouses' and 'Databases'?

What is the purpose of 'Time Travel'?

options.b: To access and recover data that was modified or deleted within a specified time window.
options.c: To archive data to long-term storage.

text: Which Snowflake component handles 'Transaction Management' and 'Metadata'?
What happens if a multi-cluster warehouse is set to 'Auto-scale' and load increases?

options.a: It increases the T-shirt size of the cluster.
options.b: It automatically starts additional clusters of the same size to handle concurrency.
options.c: It moves queries to the cloud services layer.
options.d: It notifies the admin to manually add clusters.

text: True or False: Data shared via a 'Share' object is read-only for the consumer account.

Which of the following describes the 'Economy' scaling policy for multi-cluster warehouses?

options.a: Starts clusters immediately to prevent queuing.
options.b: Only starts a new cluster if it estimates there is enough load to keep it busy for at least 6 minutes.
options.c: Limits the warehouse to X-Small size.
options.d: Only uses one cluster regardless of load.

text: True or False: You can apply a 'Network Policy' at the individual user level.
What is the primary function of 'Resource Monitors'?

options.b: To limit and monitor the consumption of credits by virtual warehouses.
options.c: To track the size of the database storage.

text: Which Snowflake edition is required for 'PrivateLink' and HIPAA compliance?
True or False: Data shared via 'Private Sharing' is encrypted in transit.
Which Snowflake component handles 'Query Parsing' and 'Optimization'?
What happens if a warehouse size is changed (e.g., Small to Medium) while queries are running?

options.b: The existing queries continue on the original resources, and new queries use the new resources.
options.d: All queries are moved to the new resources immediately.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Object Tagging' for tracking costs and sensitive data.
What is the function of a 'Stream' in Snowflake?

options.a: To load data continuously into a stage.
options.b: To track Change Data Capture (CDC) information for a table.
options.c: To connect to a streaming service like Kafka.
options.d: To process real-time analytics on query history.

text: Which Snowflake interface is a command-line tool?

options.b: It improves query performance by enabling better data pruning.
options.c: It allows more users to connect simultaneously.
options.d: It encrypts the data at the cluster level.

text: Which role is the most privileged in a Snowflake account?

options.a: It is automatically resized to X-Small.

options.d: The billing moves to an on-demand model.

options.a: To encrypt the data on disk.
options.b: To obscure sensitive data in results based on user role without changing underlying data.
options.c: To compress data for faster searches.

options.a: It updates the structure and keeps the data.
